stem: Lines $p$ and $q$ are parallel. A transversal crosses line $p$ at point $A$, making a $48°$ angle with line $p$. The transversal crosses line $q$ at point $B$. Point $C$ lies between the parallel lines, forming triangle $ABC$ with angle $ACB = (2x + 6)°$. The exterior angle of the triangle at vertex $B$ is $(4x - 10)°$. What is the measure of the interior angle of the triangle at vertex $B$?
explanation: By alternate interior angles, the angle at $A$ inside the triangle is $48°$. By the exterior angle theorem: $4x - 10 = 48 + (2x + 6)$, so $4x - 10 = 2x + 54$, giving $2x = 64$, $x = 32$. The exterior angle at $B$ is $4(32) - 10 = 118°$. The interior angle at $B$ is $180° - 118° = 62°$.

distractorRationale.C: Found the exterior angle $118°$ instead of the interior angle.

imageDescription: Two horizontal parallel lines labeled p (top) and q (bottom). A transversal crosses both lines. The angle at the intersection with p is labeled 48°. Point C is between the two parallel lines forming a triangle with the intersection points A and B.
